name: sec-review
description: Cybersecurity review plugin covering web services, servers, browser extensions, Rust projects, Go projects, shell scripts (bash/sh/dash/ksh), Android apps, iOS apps, desktop Linux, desktop macOS, desktop Windows, Kubernetes manifests, Terraform/Pulumi IaC, GitHub Actions workflows, and virtualization / alternative-container-runtime configuration (Docker daemon, Podman / Quadlet, libvirt / QEMU / KVM, Apple Containers, UTM) via a pipelined nineteen-agent architecture (sec-expert, finding-triager, cve-enricher, sast-runner, dast-runner, webext-runner, rust-runner, android-runner, ios-runner, linux-runner, macos-runner, windows-runner, k8s-runner, iac-runner, gh-actions-runner, virt-runner, go-runner, shell-runner, report-writer) with model pinning — sonnet-grade code reasoning, haiku-grade CVE feed I/O and eight-lane tool dispatch. Multi-stack dispatch is the default: when a project satisfies ≥2 inventory keys (Tauri apps = Rust + webext + desktop OS; Flutter apps = Android + iOS; React-Native apps = Android + iOS + webext), all applicable runners dispatch in parallel and findings render in separate per-lane sections. The `/sec-review` slash command accepts `--only=<lanes>` / `--skip=<lanes>` flags for selective dispatch; the final report includes a per-lane summary table and Lane-filter-applied metadata. Citation-grounded expertise across databases, frameworks, webservers (incl. Windows/IIS), proxies, frontend, browser extensions (Chrome MV3 + Firefox AMO), Rust/Cargo ecosystem + unsafe surface, Android (manifest + data + runtime), iOS (Info.plist + data + codesign), desktop Linux (systemd + sandboxing + packaging), desktop macOS (hardened runtime + TCC + .pkg/Sparkle), desktop Windows (PE hardening + Authenticode + AppLocker/WDAC + MSI/MSIX), auth, TLS, containers, secrets, and supply-chain — all primary-source-cited. Live CVE enrichment from NVD 2.0, OSV.dev (native for crates.io/Maven/NuGet/PyPI/npm/Go/RubyGems/Packagist; best-effort via GHSA for CocoaPods/SwiftPM/Debian), and GHSA with CISA KEV cross-reference. Structured skipped-list primitive distinguishes ten canonical clean-skip reasons (7 target-shape: no-apk/no-bundle/no-pkg/no-debian-source/no-elf/no-systemd-unit/no-pe; 3 host-OS-gated: requires-macos-host/requires-systemd-host/requires-windows-host; 1 profile-absent: no-notary-profile) from tool failures. `references/COVERAGE.md` is the single source of truth enumerating every lane, ecosystem, and skip reason. Per-run token-cost accounting; offline-degradation drills for CVE feeds and all eight tool lanes; prioritized markdown report with verbatim-quoted primary-source fixes.
